name: Apple Fritters (Irish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the batter at least one hour before it is needed, using the following method: Sift together the flour and salt. Make a well in the center. Add the cooled melted butter and some of the water and egg yolks. Work in the flour until smooth. Gradually add the remaining water. Allow to stand. Just before using, beat the egg whites until stiff but not dry. Gently fold into the batter mixture.
2. Peel, core, and slice the apples (slices about 1/4 - 1/2 inch thick). Dip each apple slice into the batter.
3. Deep fry the battered apple slices in very hot oil (175-180°C) until golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
4. Serve immediately, dredged with sugar and sprinkled with lemon juice.
